title: Shared Registry Schema and Stable Identity
acceptance_criteria: RuleDescription has summary, intent_clues, tech_stack, impact, must_read_if, optional entities, and no id.
RuleDescriptionIndexItem uses stable_id as identity and exposes level, required, selectable, description.
Registry-first nodes support .fabric/rules content_ref and explicit L0/L1/L2 levels.
New schema tests pass without relying on path-depth L1 derivation for new nodes.

implementation.description: Write failing shared schema tests for registry-first rule nodes, RuleDescription without id, stable_id-only identity, and minimal RuleDescriptionIndexItem.
implementation.expected_failure: Tests fail because the schema lacks RuleDescription, content_ref, and id rejection/omission behavior.

implementation.description: Implement the minimal shared types/schemas required for those tests to pass.
Refactor schema helpers and naming to keep legacy characterization separate from new registry-first semantics.
quality_checks: Type exports remain consumable by server and CLI.
